dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, report to 18th & Broad for a male assault with head injury.
I'm Jordan with Richmond Rescue. What happened here?
You're unresponsive and bleeding from the head, is that right?
No eye opening in response to voice or touch.
He doesn't respond verbally to questions.
Minimal motor response to pain to the sternum.
He's unresponsive to voice but has minimal motor response to pain. GCS likely 7.
Airway is patent on initial evaluation.
Inserting a nasopharyngeal airway into the left nare for support.
NPA placement successful. Will attach capnography now.
Capnography reading is 30. Airway remains unassisted.
Ataxic respirations at 24 with equal bilateral chest rise and no adventitious sounds.
Strong regular bilateral radial pulses. Blood pressure is 174/114 initially.
Pulse reported initially at 122 and later at 127. Sinus tachycardia per initial EKG, later sinus rhythm.
Respirations were 24 with ataxic rhythm, later 26 with same pattern.
Blood pressure drops from 174/114 to 158/114 by 19:06.
Blood glucose was 220 initially but became 0 by 19:06, not usable thereafter.
Oxygen saturation of 100% on room air initially, 98% during transport.
Initial EKG showed sinus tachycardia, later switched to sinus rhythm as noted in ePCR.
identified a 4cm laceration to back of head oozing blood; direct pressure applied as per ePCR.
Bleeding controlled using direct pressure but laceration continues to ooze slightly.
Right arm is contracted. Patient is shivering, likely due to exposure on wet ground per ePCR.
Pupils are unequal and unreactive: left 8mm, right 4mm, as per ePCR.
Temperature noted at 103.4°F initially.
Does anyone know the patient's name or age?
He's 53. His name is Michael. He's a known opioid user.
What led to the assault?
He was pistol-whipped in an alley while intoxicated. Three attackers fled.
Known medical history?
Opioid use and dependence. Was seen at VCU ED yesterday for unknown reason.
VCU Medical Center bracelet found on his wrist indicating possible registration there.
No medications administered. Patient refuses or unable to take any per ePCR.
Transporting to VCU Medical Center with trauma alert due to possible head injury and altered mental status.
Patient is placed on Reeves board, secured, and loaded into ambulance as per ePCR.
Skin shows normal capillary refill of less than 2 seconds. No signs of cyanosis or jaundice.
Medical history includes opioid use and dependence, as noted in ePCR.
Vital signs are consistent with ePCR. No change in status during transport.
Patient is unresponsive to voice with GCS 7 and reactive to pain, as per ePCR.
Trauma alert active due to head injury and possible neurological compromise.
Patient ran a temperature of 103.4°F initially. Skin had good perfusion, 2 seconds cap refill per ePCR.
12-lead EKG showed moderate ST depression and LVH per ePCR. 4-lead monitoring performed frequently.
No medications on file and no documented allergies in ePCR. Opioid use is known from medical history.
ePCR indicates the field impression includes altered mental status with head injury and opioid dependence.
Patient is positive for Covid-19, as per ePCR documentation from receiving ED post-arrival.
Capnography used with ETCO2 of 30. Airways secured with NPA and maintained through transport.
4cm laceration in occipital area applied direct pressure per ePCR.
Patient history includes possible flu-like symptoms, but influenza not confirmed in ePCR.
All standing orders followed. Used 4-lead monitoring, ETCO2, and 12-lead per ePCR.
Patient was transferred into the trauma bay with a sheet lift. Reeves board removed per ePCR.
EKG findings unchanged during transport. Stable to 19:06 readings showing sinus rhythm with moderate ST depression.
Patient is securely fastened as per EMS protocol, and all motions were stable during transport.
VCU ED assigned, trauma alert issued. Patient ready for hospital care.
EMS documentation submitted per ePCR standards. All standing orders followed throughout.
No firearm injuries, no internal bleeding signs, per ePCR. Patient silently transported as per ePCR.
Transported emergent. No complications reported. Safely arrived at VCU as per ePCR.
EMS care completed. Vitals stable with 103.4°F temp and 24-26 respirations per documentation.
